checks.name: Catalog Documentation
checks.description: The CatalogInformation/README.md is the storefront of your solution on dataminer.services. It must include a clear value proposition, key features (max 5), use cases, prerequisites (version ranges, not pinned), and 1–3 screenshots. Contact details and personal email addresses are forbidden.

checks.name: Source README
checks.description: A developer-oriented README at the repository root covering architecture overview, getting started, project structure, and contribution guide. Distinct from the Catalog README — targets contributors and integrators, not end users.

checks.name: Release Notes
checks.description: Every release must have release notes written in plain language for non-technical audiences. Notes describe what changed and why it matters — not just a list of commits or ticket numbers.

checks.name: SDM Compliance
checks.description: The Standard Data Model (SDM) defines a 3-tier architecture: T1 Foundational (SdmObject<T> models + repository storage), T2 Encapsulated (IApiHelper interface + class accepting IConnection), T3 Federated (registered via SdmRegistrar, consumable cross-solution). All three tiers must pass for a fully compliant solution.

checks.name: Storage / DOM Objects
checks.description: DOM modules must be defined using [SdmDomStorage] and [GenerateExposers] attributes, producing typed storage mappers (*.g.cs). Module IDs must be namespaced to the owning solution (e.g. infraops/assets). No solution may directly access a DOM module owned by another solution — cross-solution data must flow through the owning solution's typed IApiHelper.

checks.name: SRM Resource Types
checks.description: Solutions that model bookable services must define Profile Definitions with parameters per resource capability and Resource Pool types linked to SDM entities. Resources must be named following the SOLCODE naming convention (e.g. 'IFO Encoder'). Only relevant for solutions that orchestrate services — others are N/A.

checks.name: Inter-Solution References
checks.description: Cross-solution dependencies must flow through typed NuGet API helpers — never through direct DOM access to another solution's module. All consumed SDM packages must be declared in Directory.Packages.props. The dependency graph must be known, documented, and free of cycles. This is not new architecture — it is the natural outcome of SDM T3 compliance.

checks.name: DOM Module Boundaries
checks.description: No solution may directly access a DOM module owned by another solution. Cross-solution data must flow through the owning solution's typed API helper. Any direct DomHelper or ICrudHelper access to an externally-owned module is a major violation.

checks.name: Typed API Objects
checks.description: The solution must expose an IApiHelper interface with typed repository properties (IBulkRepository<T>, IObservableRepository<T>) — one per domain object. Consumers work with typed objects without touching DOM storage directly. This is SDM T2 compliance applied at the API surface level.

checks.name: GQI & User-Defined APIs
checks.description: GQI ad-hoc data sources expose objects for DataMiner dashboards and low-code apps. User-Defined API (UDAPI) endpoints expose objects for external HTTP consumers and AI agents. Both are required for a solution to be considered fully externally consumable.

checks.name: NuGet Devpack Published
checks.description: The solution's typed API helper (IApiHelper + models) must be published as a NuGet package to the internal Skyline feed. This allows other solutions to take a typed dependency without referencing source code. Without a NuGet devpack, cross-solution consumption requires code duplication.

checks.name: Component Privacy
checks.description: Only the main solution package may be publicly visible in the DataMiner Catalog. Sub-components used exclusively within the solution (individual scripts, add-ons, demo data, internal installers) must be marked private in their manifest.yml using registrationOptions.isHidden: true. Sub-components appearing publicly in the Catalog clutter the marketplace and confuse operators.

checks.name: Naming Convention
checks.description: All catalog item components follow Skyline naming conventions: Automation scripts use SOLCODE-TYPE-name (hyphen separator), user-visible components (apps, dashboards, elements) use SOLCODE name (space separator). SOLCODE is a 3-letter uppercase code unique to the solution. This is an existing published guideline, not a new requirement.

checks.name: Automated Tests
checks.description: Unit tests using DOM mocks (DomConnectionMock from Skyline.DataMiner.Utils.DOM) or repository interface stubs must cover core business logic. CI must run tests on every PR. Tests should run offline — without a live DataMiner system — to give fast, reliable feedback.

checks.name: CI/CD Pipeline
checks.description: A GitHub Actions pipeline must: build on every PR, run all tests automatically, and publish the NuGet or DataMiner package on a release tag. The pipeline on main must be green. This is a standard Skyline GitHub guideline, not optional.

checks.name: Dependency Health
checks.description: All NuGet packages must be up to date with no deprecated packages in use. Solutions consuming shared SDM packages (e.g. Skyline.DataMiner.SDM.Ticketing) must be on consistent versions across the landscape to avoid runtime conflicts when solutions interact.

checks.name: Health & Freshness
checks.description: A healthy solution has commits within the last 3 months, a green CI pipeline, a manageable open-issue count, and regular releases. Stale solutions signal abandonment or accumulating technical debt. All data is sourced from the GitHub API — no code scanning needed.

checks.name: Low-Code App
checks.description: A DataMiner low-code app must be present, functional, and named after the solution (SOLNAME). Operators should be able to use it without training: clear navigation, consistent layout, proper use of DataMiner style guide components. Named following SOLNAME convention per Skyline naming guidelines.

checks.name: Demo / Mock Mode
checks.description: Mock demo data injected through the solution's typed API allows POs, prospects, and developers to evaluate the solution without a live system or real data. Mocked data covers typical use cases and is toggled via the API — not hardcoded in scripts. Currently the highest-value gap across all solutions.

checks.name: LCA UX Audit
checks.description: The DataMiner low-code app must pass a full UX audit: DataMiner style guide compliance, consistent spacing and typography, proper status chips, empty states, and loading indicators. Run the dataminer-ux-audit agent for a full scored report with annotated screenshots.

checks.name: Product Owner
checks.description: A named Product Owner must be identified for each solution, tracked via a .github/CODEOWNERS file. The PO is accountable for roadmap, backlog, and release decisions. This aligns with Skyline's product management practice and ensures every solution has a clear voice for prioritisation.

checks.name: Active Backlog
checks.description: GitHub Issues or Projects must be used as an active backlog. Issues should be labeled, triaged within 2 weeks, and regularly updated. A visible roadmap (milestones or GitHub Projects) shows the solution has direction. The issue-triage agent helps maintain backlog hygiene.

checks.name: Catalog Published
checks.description: The solution must be published in the dataminer.services Catalog as 'Standard Solution' type with semantic versioning. The main range must be tagged. Regular releases (not just one initial publish) show the solution is actively evolving and supported.
